key_highlights: Global tech sell-off intensifies led by AI and chip stocks, with major indices mixed (Dow up, S&P 500 and Nasdaq down).
Oil prices fall below $75 for the first time since March as Hormuz traffic recovers after peace deal, easing supply disruption fears.
Dollar jumps to highest since November on Fed rate hike bets, pressuring EM assets and commodities.
Alan Greenspan, former Fed chair, dies at 100, marking end of an era in central banking.
SpaceX stock tumbles 23% from highs amid broader tech rout and post-IPO fade; company plans $25B bond debut.
Chubb CEO warns of threat disrupting global oil supply, while Treasury allows Iran oil sales for 60 days, creating mixed signals.
Tesla on autopilot crash kills one, reigniting safety scrutiny on autonomous driving.
Korea AI rout revives scrutiny of $290 billion levered-ETF boom, adding to systemic risk concerns.

sectors.trend: Sharp sell-off with broad-based declines in AI and chip stocks, including Intel, Lam Research, KLA, ASML, and Marvell. Alphabet also fell on DeepMind losing a Nobel winner to Anthropic. The sell-off is driven by valuation concerns, hawkish Fed, and profit-taking after a long rally.
sectors.implications: Near-term volatility likely to persist; investors should reassess AI exposure and consider selective accumulation on further weakness. The sector remains structurally strong but faces a correction.

sectors.trend: Oil prices fell below $74 a barrel as Hormuz traffic recovers after a peace deal, easing supply fears. However, Chubb CEO warns of ongoing disruption threats, and Trump's call for Iran crop sales met with skepticism. Natural gas also dropped on cooler outlooks and new pipeline capacity.
sectors.implications: Short-term bearish for oil due to easing geopolitical risk, but supply threats remain. Energy stocks may underperform near-term but could rebound on any escalation. Tidewater and Valaris are positioned for energy security themes.

sectors.trend: Dollar strength and Fed rate hike bets dominate. JPMorgan lifts PT on Western Digital, launches new credit funds with monthly redemptions. Morgan Stanley caps private credit fund after 11.6% exit request. Treasuries see good auction demand as rate-hike bets trimmed.
sectors.implications: Banks benefit from higher rates but face credit risk. Private credit liquidity concerns emerge. Financials may be a relative safe haven in a sell-off, but rate-sensitive names could be volatile.

sectors.trend: Campbell's, Coke/Pepsi/Dr Pepper using QR codes for ingredients. Summer sales events kick off as inflation squeezes consumers. Outback Steakhouse rival closes 24 locations. Consumer spending remains pressured.
sectors.implications: Defensive staples may hold up better, but discretionary retail faces headwinds. Early Prime Day deals indicate retailers pushing promotions to drive volume.

sectors.trend: FDA launches pilot to speed up early-stage trials. Food Allergy Science Initiative and Vie Ventures collaborate. Charles River Laboratories performance mixed. Pfizer not planning big M&A but focusing internally.
sectors.implications: Biotech innovation continues, but sector performance is mixed. Regulatory tailwinds for clinical trials are positive. Pfizer's conservative M&A stance may limit upside.

sectors.trend: EM assets sink as strong dollar and Korea tech rout hit markets. Argentine economy grew more than expected in Q1. Pakistan assures Iran of support. Trump says Iran's unfrozen funds remain under US control.
sectors.implications: EM underperform due to dollar strength and tech sell-off. Selective opportunities in Argentina and energy-linked EM, but overall cautious stance warranted.

sectors.trend: Bitcoin 'resilient' after hawkish Fed but no return of demand. Ether-linked nonprofit cuts budget 40%. Ex-Trump advisor makes bold case for Bitcoin. Fidelity Bitcoin Fund vs. iShares Ethereum ETF comparison emerges.
sectors.implications: Crypto remains range-bound with limited upside catalysts. Institutional interest persists but retail demand weak. ETFs provide easier access but may not drive immediate price appreciation.

sectors.trend: Camden Property Trust underperforming real estate sector. Canadian housing affordability concerns drive interest in cheaper cities. Ontario nuclear plants could cost $300B.
sectors.implications: REITs face headwinds from higher rates. Residential REITs may benefit from housing shortage but affordability is a drag. Infrastructure REITs could gain from energy transition spending.

key_themes: Global tech/AI sell-off and valuation correction
Oil price decline on Hormuz peace deal but supply threats persist
Dollar strength and Fed rate hike expectations
EM and commodity pressure from strong dollar
Private credit liquidity concerns (Morgan Stanley cap, levered-ETF scrutiny)
Consumer spending squeeze and promotional retail environment
Central bank transition (Greenspan death, Bank of Canada capital rules)

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce overweight in high-multiple AI stocks; increase cash or defensive sectors; consider value or dividend growth names.
Hedge currency exposure; favor dollar-denominated assets; reduce EM and commodity exposure; consider floating-rate bonds.
Maintain some energy exposure as hedge; monitor Hormuz traffic and Iran developments; consider energy service stocks like Tidewater.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Private Credit and Levered-ETF Systemic Risk
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to levered ETFs and private credit funds; increase liquidity; monitor Morgan Stanley cap and Korea ETF scrutiny.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Autonomous Vehicle Safety and Regulatory Risk
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to Tesla and autonomous driving plays; monitor regulatory developments; consider short-term hedges.
Favor consumer staples over discretionary; monitor retail sales and early Prime Day trends; consider defensive names like Campbell's.
Reduce EM equity and bond exposure; focus on quality EM with strong fundamentals (e.g., Argentina); hedge currency risk.

opportunities.recommendation: Selectively accumulate high-quality AI/semiconductor stocks on further weakness
opportunities.rationale: The tech sell-off is likely overdone for names with strong fundamentals (e.g., ASML, Lam Research, Marvell). Long-term AI demand remains intact. Use dollar-cost averaging.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase exposure to energy service stocks benefiting from energy security
opportunities.rationale: Despite oil price decline, energy security concerns persist. Tidewater and Valaris are well-positioned for offshore drilling and energy infrastructure demand.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider dividend growth and defensive value stocks
opportunities.rationale: In a volatile market, dividend growers like those in the Argus Dividend Growth Model provide income and stability. Campbell's and Alliant Energy are examples.

opportunities.recommendation: Explore early Prime Day deals for consumer discretionary exposure
opportunities.rationale: Retailers are aggressively promoting, which could boost near-term sales for companies like Amazon and Walmart. Monitor for bargain opportunities.

opportunities.recommendation: Look at cybersecurity stocks on pullback (Varonis, Hack The Box)
opportunities.rationale: Cyber threats remain elevated, and Varonis is weighing a sale. Hack The Box expanding platform. Sector has long-term growth tailwinds.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to high-multiple tech and AI stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: The sell-off may have further to run as valuations compress. Take profits or trim positions to lock in gains.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash or short-term Treasuries allocation
defensive_moves.rationale: With rate hikes possible and market volatility, cash provides optionality. Treasuries saw good auction demand, indicating safe-haven appeal.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against dollar strength with currency-hedged EM or commodity ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Dollar at highs will continue to pressure EM and commodities. Use hedged products to mitigate FX risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to levered ETFs and private credit
defensive_moves.rationale: Systemic risk is rising as seen in Korea levered-ETF scrutiny and Morgan Stanley capping fund. Avoid these products.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid Tesla and autonomous driving stocks pending safety review
defensive_moves.rationale: The fatal crash on autopilot will likely trigger regulatory scrutiny and negative sentiment. Avoid until clarity emerges.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Bearish with elevated volatility. Tech sell-off likely to continue as valuations adjust and Fed remains hawkish. Dollar strength will pressure EM and commodities. Oil may stabilize around $70-75 but geopolitical risks remain. Defensive sectors (staples, utilities, healthcare) may outperform. Investors should focus on quality and income.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously optimistic. AI and tech fundamentals remain strong; the correction will create buying opportunities. Energy security theme will persist. Fed rate cuts possible in H2 2027 if inflation moderates. EM could recover once dollar peaks. SpaceX bond debut and potential IPO of other tech unicorns may revive risk appetite.
key_catalysts: Fed rate decision and dot plot in July 2026
Iran oil sales implementation and Hormuz traffic normalization
Tech earnings season (especially AI names) in July
SpaceX bond pricing and potential IPO updates
US consumer spending data and inflation reports
watch_list: Dollar index (DXY) and Fed rate hike probabilities
WTI crude oil price and Hormuz shipping data
Nasdaq 100 and SOX index for tech sell-off depth
Vix index for market fear gauge
EM currency and equity ETF flows
SpaceX stock price and bond market reception
